command_launcher.apps_description: Open another Tuturuuu app from this workspace.
command_launcher.empty_description: Try a workspace, app, page, or a closer spelling.
command_launcher.search_hint: Type a workspace, app, page, acronym, or close spelling.
command_launcher.title: Command Launcher

app_category_descriptions.ai: AI apps for assistants, simulations, and creative thinking.
app_category_descriptions.all: Every routable Tuturuuu app available from this launcher.
app_category_descriptions.content: Content apps for publishing, files, and delivery.
app_category_descriptions.core: Core entrypoints for the Tuturuuu workspace platform.
app_category_descriptions.developer: Developer utilities, gateways, and technical tools.
app_category_descriptions.learning: Learning apps for courses, practice, and teaching.
app_category_descriptions.operations: Operations apps for money, inventory, and commerce.
app_category_descriptions.productivity: Productivity apps for schedule, tasks, messages, and meetings.

common.confirm_delete_description: Are you sure you want to delete this item? This action cannot be undone.

finance-recurring.amount_description: Use a negative amount for expenses and a positive amount for income.
finance-recurring.amount_invalid: Amount must be a valid number
finance-recurring.delete_description: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.
finance-recurring.end_date_description: Leave empty to keep this schedule running indefinitely.
finance-recurring.name: Name
finance-recurring.page_description: Manage automatic income and expense schedules that feed your wallets and categories.
finance-recurring.title: Recurring transactions

teach.familyBody: Publish handoff notes that make sense beside Learn progress, parent views, and the central workspace record.
teach.footer: Teach stays light: plan in context, manage in Tuturuuu, and send learners to Learn.
teach.handoffBody: Open learner previews with the same education workspace context instead of guessing what students will see.
teach.heroLead: Teach is the teacher-facing app for course user groups, classroom signals, and the daily loop between modules, attendance, reports, and metrics.
teach.heroPanelBody: Public landing stays here. After sign-in, teachers enter a workspace dashboard backed by the central Tuturuuu account.
teach.planBody: Sketch the next class loop, then jump into apps/web for the real workspace objects.
teach.signalBody: Keep attention on what needs teaching action, not another dashboard copy.
teach.studioBody: Course user groups, modules, attendance, reports, and metrics stay framed as one teaching workspace.
teach.syncBody: Teach forwards protected API traffic to apps/web so workspace ownership stays centralized.

teachDashboard.emptyGroupsBody: Create workspace user groups in apps/web first. Teach will present each group as a course group with modules, attendance, reports, and metrics.

attendance.body: Open the centralized attendance surface for the selected workspace groups.
attendance.title: Attendance pulse

groups.body: Use workspace user groups as course cohorts with a clear teaching owner.
groups.title: Course groups

metrics.body: Review score metrics before they become learner and parent-facing progress.
metrics.title: Metrics lab

modules.body: Keep lesson sequence, module coverage, and next steps tied to the group.
modules.title: Module map

preview.body: Open the learner app to inspect how the course path appears outside Teach.
preview.title: Learner preview

reports.body: Prepare reporting loops from the same groups, attendance, and score metrics.
reports.title: Report studio

featureGrid.lead: Six high-frequency teaching paths stay visible so Teach feels like a classroom operations dashboard, not a landing page.
featureGrid.title: Course group command center

teachDashboard.groupsLead: In apps/web these are workspace user groups. In Teach, treat each one as a course group with learners, modules, attendance, reports, and metrics.
teachDashboard.lead: Manage the teaching loop without turning Teach into a second platform admin. The dashboard focuses on groups, modules, centralized attendance, reports, and score metrics.

attendance.body: Reconcile attendance with the current course group before class context goes stale.
attendance.title: Attendance queue

metrics.body: Review the score metrics that will shape learner progress and parent-facing reports.
metrics.title: Metrics review

plan.body: Check module coverage and prepare the next lesson sequence for each active course group.
plan.title: Module planning

reports.body: Prepare report notes while the group status, attendance, and scores are still nearby.
reports.title: Report prep

teachDashboard.operationsLead: A focused board for the next teaching actions that usually happen after opening course groups.
teachDashboard.title: Run today’s teaching dashboard.

attendance.body: Use centralized attendance for the group, then keep the result tied to the same workspace record.
attendance.title: Check attendance once

groups.body: Start with workspace user groups and read them as dedicated course groups inside Teach.
groups.title: Own the course group

metrics.body: Scores in the classroom map to platform metrics, so reporting stays consistent across Teach, Learn, and apps/web.
metrics.title: Review score metrics

modules.body: Attach the lesson sequence to the group so students see the right module path in Learn.
modules.title: Sequence modules

teachDashboard.realtimeLead: Top 5 active courses — students, pending grading, unsubmitted work, and low scorers at a glance.
teachDashboard.statsUnavailableBody: Try again shortly to see pending grading, missing submissions, and low-scorer alerts.

teachStudentPerformance.noStudentsHint: Add learners to this course group to see their performance.
teachStudentPerformance.quizSubtitle: module quiz practice · tests tracked separately
teachStudentPerformance.title: Student Performance

context.hint: Optional. Add the class level, learning goals, or any constraints the AI should follow.
context.label: Teacher context
context.placeholder: For example: Focus on beginner-friendly explanations and include review questions after each module.

aiGenerate.description: Upload a PDF, Word document, or text file. AI will create structured modules, quizzes, and flashcards.

dropzone.description: PDF, Word (.docx), or plain text · max {maxSize} MB
dropzone.title: Drop a file here or click to browse

errors.fileTooLarge: File is too large ({fileSize}). Maximum size is {maxSize} MB.
errors.unsupportedFileType: Unsupported file type. Please upload a PDF, Word document, or text file.

status.analyzing: AI is analyzing your document and creating course content...

success.description: The new modules have been added to this course.
success.title: Modules generated

aiGenerate.title: Generate modules with AI

teachModules.allCoursesDescription: Each course below is a Teach-owned workspace user group. Draft courses stay hidden from Learn until published.
teachModules.createTestDescription: Create a new test for this course by choosing which modules to include lessons from.
teachModules.description: Create and manage course groups in Teach. Publish a course when learners should see its published modules in Learn.
teachModules.editTestDescription: Modify the test's details including name, start date and time, duration, and instructions.
teachModules.invalidDuration: Duration must be between 1 and 1440 minutes
teachModules.invalidManualScore: Enter a score between 0 and the question maximum.
teachModules.learningObjectivesDescription: This assessment supports the learning outcomes covered in the following modules:

lesson.attachFilesDescription: Select files from the workspace drive to attach to this lesson.
lesson.saveFailed: Failed to save lesson. Please try again.

teachModules.loadError: Failed to load courses. Please try again.
teachModules.noAssociatedModules: No modules are explicitly associated with this test.
teachModules.noCourses: No courses found. Create the first draft course here in Teach.
teachModules.noInstructions: No instructions provided for this test.
teachModules.noSubmissionsDescription: Students haven't started this test yet.
teachModules.noQuizSubmissionsDescription: Learners haven't answered any quiz questions in this lesson yet.
teachModules.quizSubmissionsDescription: Review learner responses for this lesson's quiz set.
teachModules.scoresVisibleToStudents: Students can see their scores after submitting.
teachModules.startTimeInPast: Start date and time must be in the future

storage.description: View uploaded files and folders for this course group.
storage.title: Course storage

teachModules.testDescriptionPlaceholder: e.g. Read the questions carefully and complete all sections before the time limit.
teachModules.testNotFoundDescription: The requested test could not be found in this course.
teachModules.testsLoadError: Failed to load tests. Please try again.
teachModules.title: Course Modules

assignments.lead: Create course posts and assignment notes without leaving Teach.
assignments.title: Assignments and posts

attendance.lead: Save daily attendance for enrolled learners in the selected course.
attendance.title: Attendance

teachOperations.calendarHelp: Scheduled class days are highlighted from the course schedule. The color changes after attendance is saved.
teachOperations.dayTooltipNoClass: No class is scheduled for this day.
teachOperations.dayTooltipScheduled: Class is scheduled. Attendance has not been saved yet.
teachOperations.emptyCoursesBody: Create a draft course first, then return here to manage classroom operations.
teachOperations.generatedSessionsDescription: {count} class days will be available for attendance.
teachOperations.learnerAssignmentPreviewLead: Published course posts appear in Learn as learner assignments.

metrics.lead: Create indicators and save learner metric values for the selected course.
metrics.title: Metrics

teachOperations.noReportsYetBody: Create a learner report and it will appear here for preview and follow-up.
teachOperations.noSessionBody: Pick class days in the schedule tool. Teach will only allow attendance on scheduled class days.
teachOperations.previewEmptyContent: The report body preview will appear here.
teachOperations.previewEmptyFeedback: Learner feedback preview will appear here.
teachOperations.reportComposerLead: Draft once, preview exactly what the learner-facing report will communicate, then save it to the course.

reports.lead: Write course reports for enrolled learners from inside Teach.
reports.title: Reports

teachOperations.scheduleLead: Set the class term and recurring weekdays. Teach seeds these as 7:00-8:00 AM GMT+7 sessions for attendance.

version-badge.description: Build and deployment details for this app.
version-badge.setting_description: Show a compact build/version badge in the bottom-right corner of supported apps.
version-badge.title: Version details

workspace-invitation.description: Accept this invitation to join the workspace. App permissions will be checked after you join.
workspace-invitation.title: Join {workspace}

ws-debt-loan.counterparty_too_long: Counterparty must be 255 characters or fewer
ws-debt-loan.description_too_long: Description must be 500 characters or fewer

ws-quizzes.additional_context_placeholder: For example: focus on coding examples or make the assessment more challenging.

ws-quizzes.load_error: Failed to load quizzes. Please try again.
ws-quizzes.multiple_choice_min_options: Add at least two non-empty options before saving.
ws-quizzes.paragraph_response_area_hint: Students will type their answer here. No predefined correct answer is required.
ws-quizzes.question: Question
ws-quizzes.test_questions_manager_description: Prepare and configure assessment questions manually or using AI for each linked course module.
